gate_question: Does this exact current protocol, with current live state, allow an unprivileged attacker to extract funds or rewards beyond entitlement?

candidate.title_or_claim: Sandwich MEV on swapTitanXForTorusAndBuild drains protocol TitanX to genesisWallet while attacker captures AMM price difference
candidate.claimed_root_cause: 10% TWAP-based slippage tolerance in _swapTitanXForTorus combined with 10% liquiditySlippage in _addLiquidityBuild allows an attacker to front-run the pool, cause the protocol to receive fewer Torus than fair price, leave material TitanX as leftover sent to genesisWallet, and back-run for profit
candidate.claimed_impact: Attacker extracts sandwich profit from AMM; protocol loses TitanX value efficiency; genesisWallet receives non-dust TitanX leftovers instead of those funds being used for LP

live_preconditions.name: lastTitanXBuildIntervalNumber (current interval amountBurned == 0)
live_preconditions.observed_value: 53061 (amountBurned state for this interval not directly captured)

live_preconditions.name: TitanX/TORUS pool liquidity depth (determines cost to move spot 10%)
live_preconditions.command_if_missing: cast call <TORUS_TOKEN> 'titanXTorusPool()(address)' --rpc-url mainnet && cast call <POOL> 'liquidity()(uint128)' --rpc-url mainnet

source_code_basis.why_it_matters: amountOutMinimum is set to TWAP quote * 90%; a same-block front-run does not move the TWAP, so the attacker can push spot price up to 10% above TWAP without causing a revert, resulting in fewer Torus returned
half TitanX is swapped for Torus (at manipulated price), remainder TitanX + fewer-than-expected Torus are passed to _addLiquidityBuild; no check on torusPurchased vs. expected before LP call

amount0Min/amount1Min are 90% of desired; at manipulated pool price the LP absorbs less TitanX per unit of Torus; leftover TitanX (amount0 - used0 or amount1 - used1) is transferred to genesisWallet, not returned to protocol or burned
15-minute TWAP oracle; same-block manipulation does not affect TWAP, so amountOutMinimum is computed from pre-manipulation price, providing no protection against within-tolerance spot manipulation

attacker_path.actor: Any unprivileged Ethereum address with sufficient TitanX capital to move the TitanX/TORUS pool spot price by up to ~10% relative to the 15-minute TWAP
attacker_inputs: Front-run tx: ISwapRouter.exactInput TitanX->TORUS on the TitanX/TORUS pool, moving spot price up by 8-9% vs TWAP
Back-run tx: ISwapRouter.exactInput TORUS->TitanX on the same pool at the elevated price
call_sequence: 1. Attacker submits front-run: large TitanX->TORUS swap, spot price rises ~8-9% vs TWAP
2. swapTitanXForTorusAndBuild executes: _swapTitanXForTorus(half) returns fewer TORUS (within 10% tolerance, no revert)
3. _addLiquidityBuild(remainder, fewerTorus): LP absorbs less TitanX at manipulated ratio; leftover TitanX sent to genesisWallet
4. Attacker submits back-run: sells TORUS back for TitanX at elevated price, capturing sandwich profit
state_before: TitanX/TORUS pool spot price near TWAP
state_after: genesisWallet TitanX balance increased by material leftover (beyond dust)
Attacker net TitanX balance positive after gas
Protocol received fewer TORUS per TitanX than fair TWAP price

extraction_analysis.attacker_gain: Sandwich profit: attacker buys TORUS cheap (pre-manipulation), sells at elevated price after victim tx executes at worse rate; net gain is the price impact captured minus gas
extraction_analysis.victim_or_protocol_loss: Protocol loses TitanX value efficiency: same TitanX allocation produces fewer TORUS and less LP value; genesisWallet receives non-dust TitanX that should have been used for LP
extraction_analysis.why_gain_exceeds_entitlement: Attacker is not a participant in the build flow and has no entitlement to any portion of the protocol's TitanX allocation; the sandwich extracts value from the protocol's AMM interaction by exploiting the 10% slippage window that the TWAP oracle cannot close

setup: Fork Ethereum mainnet at block 25530134 (or nearest block where liquidityAddedBuild == true and a fresh titanXIntervalsBuild interval is available with amountBurned == 0)
Fund attacker address with 10x interval allocation in TitanX via deal()
Record genesisWallet TitanX balance before attack
Record attacker TitanX balance before attack
transaction_sequence: vm.prank(attacker); ISwapRouter(UNISWAP_V3_ROUTER).exactInput(...) // TitanX->TORUS, move spot ~8-9% above TWAP
expected_assertions: uint256 genesisBalanceAfter = IERC20(TITAN_X_ADDRESS).balanceOf(genesisWallet); assertGt(genesisBalanceAfter - genesisBalanceBefore, 1e18); // leftover > 1 TitanX (non-dust)
int256 attackerNet = int256(IERC20(TITAN_X_ADDRESS).balanceOf(attacker)) - int256(attackerBalanceBefore); assertGt(attackerNet, 0); // attacker net positive after gas
local_proof_required.reject_if_assertion_fails: If genesisWallet leftover is consistently below 1e18 TitanX across all manipulation sizes within the 10% tolerance, or if attacker is net-negative after gas at all realistic pool depths, reject as expected-behavior rounding dust with no material extraction
